Get the metadata for a statbank table

Source: R/statbank.R
statbank_meta.Rd

Fetches a table's title and variables: codes, display texts, and the values each variable can take.

Usage

statbank_meta(table, lang = NULL, bank = "gl")

Arguments

table

The table's path in the tree, e.g. "BE/BE01/BEXSAT1.PX". A bare table id (e.g. "BEXSAT1.PX") also works once the table list has been fetched with statbank_tables() or statbank_search() in the same session.

lang

Language of titles and labels, or NULL (default) for the bank's own default. Greenland offers "da", "kl", "en"; the Faroe Islands offer "fo", "en".

bank

Which statbank: "gl" (Greenland, the default) or "fo" (the Faroe Islands).

Value

A list with title, path, and variables, where variables is a tibble with columns code, text, values (list), valueTexts (list), elimination, and time.

See also

statbank_get()

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
meta <- statbank_meta("BE/BE01/BEXSAT1.PX")
meta$variables
} # }
